messages.content: In a master-slave flip-flop, the slave flip-flop responds to an input during the _______ edge of the clock.
rising edge
Active low PR means that Q = 1 when PR is ____.
low (or 0)
It is the minimum length of time the data bit must be present before the CLK edge hits.
Setup time.
In a master-slave flip-flop, the master flip-flop responds to an input during the _______ edge of the clock.
falling edge.
A type of clocking wherein the flip-flop produces output only on the rising (or falling) edge of the clock signal.
Edge-triggered flip-flop.
A bubble at the clock input of a flip-flop indicates what type of level or edge trigger (clocking)?
A bubble (inversion) on the clock input of a flip-flop indicates a negative edge-triggered (or falling-edge triggered) flip-flop.
Active low CLR means that Q = 0 when CLR is ____.
low (or 0).
The triangle in the clock input indicates what type of clocking?

The triangle or arrowhead in the clock input of a flip-flop indicates that the flip-flop is level triggered. This means that the output of the flip-flop changes based on the level of the clock signal, rather than just the rising or falling edge.
Given that the inputs to an SR Flip-flop using NAND Latch are given as S = 1 and R = 0, it represents the _________ state.
Given that the inputs to an SR NOR Latch are S = 1 and R = 0, it represents the _________ state.
Given that the input to a D Flip-flop using NOR Latch is 0, what will be its output on the next clock cycle?

The output of a D flip-flop using NOR latch will remain in its current state when the input is 0, regardless of the clock signal. Therefore, the output on the next clock cycle would be the same as the current output.
A/an __________ sequential logic circuit depends upon the input signals at any instant of time and the order in which the inputs change.
Asynchronous sequential logic circuit.
Given that the inputs to an SR NAND Latch are S = 0 and R = 0, it represents the _________ state.

Indeterminate or undefined state. In an SR NAND latch, when both inputs S and R are 0, it can lead to a situation where both outputs Q and Q' are constantly changing, leading to unpredictable behavior. This is an invalid state and should be avoided in practical designs.
A/an _______ circuit uses memory elements. The previous state of the circuit has effect on the present state of the circuit.
Sequential circuit.
Latches are memory elements and these are _______ sensitive devices.
Latches are level-sensitive devices. The output of a latch changes based on the level (high or low) of the input signal, rather than just the rising or falling edge of a clock signal.
Given that the inputs to an SR NAND Latch are S = 0 and R = 1, it represents the _________ state.
RESET (Q = 0, Q' = 1).
Given that the inputs to an SR Flip-flop using NAND Latch are given as S = 1 and R = 1, it represents the _________ state.

Invalid state. In an SR flip-flop using NAND latch, when both inputs S and R are 1, it can lead to a situation where both outputs Q and Q' are constantly changing, leading to unpredictable behavior. This is an invalid state and should be avoided in practical designs.
Given that the inputs to a JK Flip-flop are given as J = 1, K = 1, it represents the _______ state
Toggle or switch state. When both inputs J and K of a JK flip-flop are set to 1, the output of the flip-flop will toggle or switch states with each clock pulse.
A/an _________ circuit selects binary information from one of many input lines and directs it to a single output line.
Multiplexer (MUX).
What type of encoder is used to convert an octal number to binary number?

A 3-to-8 line encoder is typically used to convert an octal number to a binary number. The octal number has 3 bits, and it is encoded into its binary equivalent using 8 output lines. Each output line represents one of the possible binary combinations of the 3 input bits.
What type of decoder is used to convert a binary number to octal number?

A 3-to-8 line decoder is typically used to convert a binary number to an octal number. The binary number has 3 bits, and it is decoded into its octal equivalent using 8 input lines. Each input line represents one of the possible binary combinations of the 3 output bits.
What type of decoder is used to convert a binary number to hexadecimal number?

A 4-to-16 line decoder is typically used to convert a binary number to a hexadecimal number. The binary number has 4 bits, and it is decoded into its hexadecimal equivalent using 16 output lines. Each output line represents one of the possible hexadecimal combinations of the 4 input bits.
A/an _______ circuit converts any given signal to an n bit code.
Encoder.
A circuit that receives information on a single line and transmits this information to one of 2^n possible output lines.
Binary decoder.
An 8x1 MUX has how many output lines?
An 8x1 multiplexer has only 1 output line. The selected input line is directed to this single output line based on the value of the select lines.
In order to produce a sum of product in combinational logic, what gate is used?
The AND gate is used to produce a sum of products in combinational logic.
A/an ________ circuit can also be referred to as a ""data selector"".
Multiplexer (MUX).
A/an ________ circuit is used to convert decimal number to binary number.
Decimal to binary converter. One commonly used type of decimal to binary converter is the binary weighted resistor DAC (digital-to-analog converter).
